commonLiftedVolterraAtomStatus.label: common lifted Volterra atom
commonLiftedVolterraAtomStatus.reason: The plus and minus branch features differ only by the scalar factors 1+s+u and 1-s-u; the Volterra atom A(s,u), test f(s), branch weight, and measure factor are common.

liftedHardyMultiplierIdentityStatus.label: lifted Hardy multiplier identity
liftedHardyMultiplierIdentityStatus.reason: Because the common atom is shared, F_-(s,u)=kappa(s,u)F_+(s,u) with kappa(s,u)=(1-s-u)/(1+s+u) wherever s,u>=0.

pointwiseHardyMultiplierStatus.label: pointwise Hardy multiplier contraction
pointwiseHardyMultiplierStatus.reason: For s,u>=0, 1+s+u>0 and |1-s-u|<=1+s+u.  Therefore |kappa(s,u)|<=1 on the Volterra quadrant.

compressedMultiplierAlgebraStatus.label: compressed multiplier algebra
compressedMultiplierAlgebraStatus.reason: After applying the Volterra integration map C and Green lift E, the signed branch transport has the algebraic form C K E, where K is multiplication by kappa.  Contractivity of the compressed map is a separate closed-form Green theorem.

proof: Write the lifted plus feature as F_+=(1+s+u)A(s,u)f(s).
Write the lifted minus feature as F_-=(1-s-u)A(s,u)f(s).
Divide by the common factor to get F_-=kappa F_+.
The integrated branch map is therefore C K E at the algebraic level.
